evidence_refs.quote: Subword tokenizers systematically fragment certain byte sequences—numeric literals, file paths, code identifiers, and punctuation-heavy strings—into tokens that small language models struggle to reproduce verbatim. We investigate whether a pointer/cross-attention decoder that attends to an explicit byte-memory buffer of detected fragile spans can recover exact-copy performance without receiving current-byte generation-slot labels at inference time.
